text: Fʋtekle yilebwɛ. Lalahɛbwɛ mʋ́ a, fʋtekisi. Mʋ́ sʋ fʋ Wie Bulu lada fʋ ofuli, pʋ ansigyi bʋla fʋtɔ dʋn fʋ aba.
translated: You have done a bad job. You are lazy that is why you are poor. That is why your brother Bulu is richer than you, so you are jealous of him.

ref: You plan brutal crimes, and your lying words cut like a sharp razor.
text: fʋdɛ lalahɛ susu. Ofunupʋ olembiapʋ, fʋ ɔdandʋ bʋ ogya fɛ ɔdayibi.
translated: The sun rises in the east. When it rises we know that day has broken.

ref: because you protect and bless your people.
text: Nana Bulu, fʋ ɩbɩtɔ nkpahɔ bʋ. Ha fʋ oyula ɩba fʋ abisʋ. Ɩya.
translated: My God, don't abandon me. Give me life so that I will thank you. Amen.

ref: But you see the trouble and the distress, and you will do something. The poor can count on you, and so can orphans.
text: Tamɛ togyitɔ ɩda fʋ ansɩtɔ, tsufɛ fʋdɛ ɩwɩɔsɩn pʋ́ ipian awunpʋ wun. Fʋtekleá fɔ́bʋa amʋ́ bregyibrɛ. Apɔnpʋ bʋtɔpʋ ansɩ dɩnka fʋsʋ, fʋtegyi awumbi bʋalɛ bregyibrɛ.
translated: When you go to farm, you take your hoe along, and if you don't have a cutlass you use the farm matchet. You sharpen the matchet well before you go to farm, you take the matchet and sharpen it well before you go.

ref: Don't let lions eat me. You rescued me from the horns of wild bulls,
text: Lɛ mɩ tsu agyata anfɩ ɔnɔ. Tsú akpokpo anfɩ bʋbʋ ɩyɩn anfɩ nsiebi ɔnɔ.
translated: Give me some of that corn flour. Some of that corn flour is not good for making tuo.

ref: You love justice and hate evil. And so, your God chose you and made you happier than any of your friends.
text: Fʋtekle yilebwɛ. Lalahɛbwɛ mʋ́ a, fʋtekisi. Mʋ́ sʋ fʋ Wie Bulu lada fʋ ofuli, pʋ ansigyi bʋla fʋtɔ dʋn fʋ aba.
translated: You have done a bad job. You are lazy that is why you are poor. That is why your brother Bulu is richer than you, because he is hardworking and does not waste his money.

ref: You plan brutal crimes, and your lying words cut like a sharp razor.

translated: The sun rises in the east. When it rises we know it is daytime, you will see that it is shining brightly.

ref: I stuck my sword through my enemies, and they were crushed under my feet.
text: Nobwie amʋ́ bun mɩ ayabiasɩ, bʋmɛtalɩ tra kʋsʋ.
translated: The fool does not know what he is doing, he just does it.

ref: All who want me dead set traps to catch me, and those who want to harm and destroy me plan and plot all day.
text: Aha ánɩ́ bʋmɛdɛ mɩ yile kle bafɩnɩ mɩ ayibi. Mɩ lalahɛ adunkapʋ ɛ bʋdekleá bɛ́lɛ mɩ ɩnʋ. Ɔpa-onye bʋdɛ mɩ ɩwɩ agywɩɩn lalahɛ susu.
translated: The farm is not good because of the bush. The bush is dense and it chases away the farm. When it is about to rain, the bush flourishes.

ref: guide me by your truth and instruct me. You keep me safe, and I always trust you.
text: Kpa mɩ ambuo fʋ ɔnɔkwalɩ, afʋsuna mɩ atɔ, tsufɛ fʋgyi mɩ nkpa ɔhɔpʋ Bulu. Fʋ ansɩtɔ ndɛkɩ ɔyɩkɛhɛ fɛ́ɛ́.
translated: The dog chases the goat and catches it, then takes it to the Bulu shrine. It is there that it usually runs to.

ref: God shines brightly from Zion, the most beautiful city.
text: Bulu tɔwankɩ tsu Sion, wuluá ɩbʋ akɩlɛ ɔyɩtɔ fɛ́ɛ́.
translated: The enemy will flee from Zion, they will run away because of the Lord.

ref: Rescue me from enemy swords and save me from those dogs.
text: Hɔ mɩ lɛ ɔdayi ɔnɔ. Hɔ mɩ ɔkla ɔkʋkʋnʋ ɔkʋlɛ lɛ akɩaɩ anfɩ ɩbɩ.
translated: Go and bring food. Go and bring food to eat and drink.

ref: because you protect and bless your people.
text: Nana Bulu, fʋ ɩbɩtɔ nkpahɔ bʋ. Ha fʋ oyula ɩba fʋ abisʋ. Ɩya.
translated: Mr. Bulu, take this fowl and give (it) to her. When you go, give (it) to your mother-in-law.

ref: But you see the trouble and the distress, and you will do something. The poor can count on you, and so can orphans.

translated: When you go to farm, you take your hoe along, so that you will be able to hoe the weeds off the cassava farm. You should not allow the weeds to overgrow. The weeds will choke the cassava if you allow them to overgrow.

translated: Be patient, it will be well. If it is not well, be very patient. That is why your Lord Jesus took your sin and nailed it to the cross with him.

translated: Mr. Bulu, take this fowl's egg. When you get home give it to your father. Okay.

ref: But you see the trouble and the distress, and you will do something. The poor can count on you, and so can orphans.

translated: Let us go and look for some food to eat. When we get there we will share the food equally.

translated: You have done a bad job. You are lazy that's why you are poor. That's why your brother Bulu scolded you and the elders also beat you severely.
